target.name: The Nuclear Company
target.founder: Jonathan Webb, Patrick Maloney, Kiran Bhatraju

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
The Nuclear Company is a Series A frontier-tech venture in the nuclear energy sector, having raised $50M+. The relevant comp universe includes 3 public equities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies in nuclear energy, though narrow peer-stage filtering yields insufficient datapoints for robust median comparables.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised ($50M) as a base proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression yields $100M. A peer-median approach was not viable: while 8 same-sector private peers exist (including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power), filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with comparable valuation tiers produced fewer than 3 datapoints, rendering sector-wide medians unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $85M to $115M, with a median point of $100M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty: the absence of robust same-stage comparables means the 2.0x multiplier assumption cannot be validated against peer progression patterns. Key risks to valuation include stage-specific capital market conditions, technology de-risking relative to the peer set, and the early-stage nature of nuclear ventures, which may exhibit valuation volatility. Additional clarity on revenue, pre-commercial milestones, or IP defensibility would strengthen conviction in this range.
